files.patch:
@@ -618,7 +618,7 @@ {"id":"bd-eel0","title":"Harden curl|bash temp file handling (mktemp; avoid /tmp script execution)","description":"install.sh downloads helper libs (context/state/report/ubuntu_upgrade) into predictable /tmp filenames (e.g., /tmp/acfs-context-14822.sh) and run_preflight_checks may execute /tmp/acfs-preflight.sh if present. This creates a symlink/race attack surface (and can run attacker-controlled /tmp scripts). Fix by using mktemp for all downloaded helper scripts and never trusting pre-existing /tmp scripts; keep behavior identical otherwise.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-25T07:21:36.723024Z","updated_at":"2025-12-25T07:33:31.746204Z","closed_at":"2025-12-25T07:33:31.746204Z","close_reason":"Completed: use mktemp for downloaded helper scripts/tarballs and never execute /tmp preflight (commit 3c22af8)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-egf5","title":"TASK: Update /wizard/ssh-connect for password-first connection","description":"## Parent Feature agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t66c (Wizard Password-First Flow Updates) ## Summary Update the SSH Connect wizard page to use password-based connection command instead of key-based. Users will SSH as root with password for their first connection. ## Current State Primary command shown: \`ssh -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@IP\` This fails if the user didn't successfully add their SSH key to the provider. ## Target State Primary command: \`ssh root@IP\` With clear expectation that password will be requested. ## Implementation Details ### File: apps/web/app/wizard/ssh-connect/page.tsx ### Key Changes 1. **Update primary command** \`\`\`typescript const sshCommand = \`ssh root@\${vpsIP}\`; // Remove the -i flag and key path // Remove ubuntu user, use root \`\`\` 2. **Add password expectation** \`\`\`tsx When you run this command, it will ask for your password. This is the root password from your VPS provider. \`\`\` 3. **Update success indicator** Change expected prompt from \`ubuntu@vps:~$\` to \`root@vps:~#\` 4. **Update SimplerGuide** - Add step: "Enter your password when prompted" - Explain: "Nothing will appear as you type - that's normal" - Clarify: "This is the last time you'll need the password" 5. **Update troubleshooting** - "Wrong password" - check VPS provider email/panel - "Connection refused" - VPS still booting, wait 2 min - Remove SSH key specific errors from primary list ### New Password Guidance \`\`\`tsx When prompted, type your root password and press Enter.

Important: As you type, nothing will appear on screen. This is a security feature, not a bug! Just type your password and press Enter. \`\`\` ### Fallback Section Keep but reframe: > **Ubuntu user instead of root?** > Some providers use 'ubuntu' as the default user. If root doesn't work, try: > \`ssh ubuntu@IP\` > You may still need a password, or the provider might have set up a key. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Primary command is password-based (no -i flag) - [ ] Uses root user by default - [ ] Password expectation clearly stated - [ ] "Nothing appears when typing" explained - [ ] Expected prompt shows root@ - [ ] Troubleshooting updated for password flow - [ ] SimplerGuide steps updated - [ ] Mobile layout verified ## Testing 1. Follow instructions with fresh password-only VPS 2. Verify command works 3. Verify password prompt appears as described 4. Check troubleshooting covers common password issues ## Dependencies - TASK: Update /wizard/create-vps (should come first so flow is coherent) ## Files Modified - apps/web/app/wizard/ssh-connect/page.tsx","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T00:20:22.454663Z","updated_at":"2025-12-22T00:35:05.365902Z","closed_at":"2025-12-22T00:35:05.365902Z","close_reason":"Completed (page already matches password-first flow)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-egf5","depends_on_id":"bd-5yty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T00:20:50.121846Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"bd-eh383","title":"meta_skill ARM64 Linux binary missing — blocked upstream (GH #115)","description":"## Context GitHub issue #115 filed by tayiorbeii (2026-02-06). On ARM64 Linux (aarch64), meta_skill install 404s because v0.1.0 has no aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu binary. ## Available binaries in v0.1.0: - ms-0.1.0-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz (ARM64 macOS - exists) - ms-0.1.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz (x86_64 Linux - exists) - ms-0.1.0-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip (Windows - exists) - aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu — MISSING ## Two-Part Fix ### Part 1: ACFS workaround (THIS repo, can do now) Update doctor.sh check at lines 1115-1116 to detect aarch64 on Linux and show: "meta_skill: ARM64 Linux binary not yet available (see https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill/issues/1)" instead of the generic install suggestion that will 404. Implementation: ```bash # In doctor.sh, before the meta_skill install suggestion: if [[ "$(uname -m)" == "aarch64" ]] && [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Linux" ]]; then log_item "warn" "meta_skill (ms)" "ARM64 Linux binary not yet available" return 0 # Skip install attempt fi ``` ### Part 2: Upstream (meta_skill repo, blocked) Add aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu to the GoReleaser or release CI matrix in Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill. Requires: - Cross-compilation target or dedicated ARM64 runner - New release (v0.1.1 or v0.2.0) This is blocked on upstream release work. ## Testing After Part 1: 1. On this VPS (x86_64): `acfs doctor` should still show meta_skill normally 2. Simulate ARM64: mock uname output or grep the code to verify the aarch64 check exists 3. Verify the issue URL is correct and accessible ## Key Files - scripts/lib/doctor.sh (lines 1109-1117, meta_skill check) - Upstream: Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill release CI matrix ## Status Part 1 is actionable now. Part 2 is blocked upstream. Keep this bead open until both parts are done.","notes":"ACFS-side mitigation is complete: Linux ARM64 now warns correctly in doctor.sh, builds meta_skill from source in the generated installer, scripts/lib/update.sh, and scripts/lib/doctor_fix.sh, and now has regression coverage in tests/unit/test_doctor_generated.sh plus manifest and doctor-fix/update tests. Remaining work is upstream binary publishing for aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu in meta_skill.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:16:39.015874050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:54:56Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T00:54:56Z","close_reason":"Closed as locally complete: ACFS now avoids the broken ARM64 Linux installer path and has regression coverage; remaining work belongs upstream in Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill issue #1.","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup#115","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["arm64","bug","meta_skill","upstream-blocked"],"comments":[{"id":83,"issue_id":"bd-eh383","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Part 1 complete: doctor.sh updated to detect ARM64 Linux and show a helpful warning + build-from-source suggestion instead of the install script that would 404. Part 2 (upstream meta_skill binary publish) still pending.","created_at":"2026-02-11T17:11:27Z"}]} -{"id":"bd-eke1f","title":"Deep cross-agent audit of current shared edits","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T03:34:31.227273730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T03:34:31.227273730Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} +{"id":"bd-eke1f","title":"Deep cross-agent audit of current shared edits","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T03:34:31.227273730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:08:28.910100672Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:08:28.909852347Z","close_reason":"Completed deep audit; hardened checksum verification and fixed update-channel test isolation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-eli","title":"Research CASS session data format and API","description":"# Task: Research CASS session data format and API ## Context Part of EPIC: Agent Session Sharing and Replay (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0sb) ## Why This Comes First Before we can export sessions, we need to understand how CASS stores them: - What format is the session data? - What commands does CASS expose? - What fields are available for export? - Are there multiple session types (Claude vs Codex vs Gemini)? ## Research Questions 1. Run 'cass --help' and document available commands 2. Run 'cass list' and examine output format 3. Run 'cass get --json' and examine structure 4. Check if CASS stores sessions per-agent or unified 5. Identify what data is NOT stored (we can't export what doesn't exist) ## Deliverables - Document CASS CLI commands and their output - Example session data structure - List of exportable fields - Any limitations or gaps discovered ## Acceptance Criteria - CASS API documented - Session format understood - Export schema can be designed based on actual data - Gaps identified for follow-up ## Notes This is research, not implementation. Output is documentation that informs subsequent tasks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:51:13.705596Z","updated_at":"2025-12-21T20:52:01.467990Z","closed_at":"2025-12-21T20:52:01.467990Z","close_reason":"Research complete. Documented CASS session format, CLI commands, search/export APIs, session storage locations, and identified 7 limitations. Created docs/cass-session-format-research.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-eooz","title":"Add password clarification (VPS root vs provider account)","description":"# Task: Add Password Clarification ## Parent Epic [EPIC] First SSH Connection Experience (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q7r3) ## Description Users frequently confuse their VPS provider account password (Hetzner/Contabo login) with the VPS root password (set during VPS creation). This is the #1 support issue. They try the wrong password, get locked out, and panic. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/ssh-connect/page.tsx Add a PROMINENT AlertCard above the SSH command: \`\`\`tsx

When SSH asks for a password, use the VPS root password — the one you created (or received via email) when setting up the VPS.

❌ NOT this password:

Your Hetzner/Contabo/OVH account login

✓ USE this password:

The root password from VPS creation

Some providers email you the root password. Check your inbox if you didn't set one.

\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Appears PROMINENTLY, not buried in SimplerGuide - [ ] Clear visual distinction between wrong and right password - [ ] Mentions email option for some providers - [ ] Uses warning styling to grab attention - [ ] Works well on mobile (stacked layout) ## UI/UX Notes - This is SO common that it deserves main-content placement - The two-column comparison makes it instantly clear - Mobile should stack vertically - Consider adding link back to VPS creation step if they forgot","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T18:41:38.986261Z","updated_at":"2025-12-22T19:02:19.783445Z","closed_at":"2025-12-22T19:02:19.783445Z","close_reason":"Already covered: ssh-connect/page.tsx has AlertCard + Step 6 explaining VPS password vs hidden typing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-epc","title":"[codex.1] Fix doctor.sh codex auth detection","description":"## Goal Update scripts/lib/doctor.sh to correctly detect codex-cli authentication via OAuth tokens instead of checking for OPENAI_API_KEY. ## Current (Incorrect) Behavior The check_codex_auth() function currently: 1. Checks for OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable 2. Looks for OPENAI_API_KEY in ~/.zshrc.local 3. Looks for OPENAI_API_KEY in /data/projects/.envrc 4. Reports "pass" if any of these exist ## Correct Behavior Should instead: 1. Check for ~/.codex/auth.json (or similar OAuth token file) 2. Optionally verify the token isn't expired 3. Report "pass" if OAuth credentials exist 4. Suggest running "codex auth" if not authenticated ## Technical Notes - Need to verify the actual path where codex-cli stores OAuth tokens - The token file location may vary by OS/version - Common locations: ~/.codex/, ~/.config/codex/, ~/.local/share/codex/ ## Files to Modify - scripts/lib/doctor.sh: Update check_codex_auth() function ## Acceptance Criteria 1. No mention of OPENAI_API_KEY in codex auth check 2. Correctly detects OAuth authentication 3. Provides helpful guidance for unauthenticated users 4. Regenerate scripts/generated/doctor_checks.sh after changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:25:35.325519Z","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:37:13.931458Z","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:37:13.931458Z","close_reason":"Fixed check_codex_auth to detect OAuth tokens in ~/.codex/auth.json instead of checking OPENAI_API_KEY env var. Also cleaned up redundant shell redirects.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-epc","depends_on_id":"bd-ua5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:26:35.689670Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-epc","depends_on_id":"bd-wy1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:26:37.126029Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
